content.problemStatement:
Write a program to calculate the sum of the digits present in an alphanumeric string.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer, T, denoting the number of test cases.
Each of the next T lines contains a string consisting of lowercase/uppercase English letters, along with digits 0 to 9. (There shall be no space in the string)


Output Format

For each test case, print the sum of digits in each string.
constraints: String contains only letters (a‑z, A‑Z) and digits (0‑9)
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty alphanumeric string (letters a‑z, A‑Z and digits 0‑9) without spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the sum of all digit characters that appear in the corresponding string. Print each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First string digits 1+2+3=6. Second string has no digits, sum is 0.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the given string character by character. Whenever the character is between '0' and '9' we convert it to its numeric value by subtracting the character code of '0' and add it to a running sum. After processing the whole string we output the sum. This linear scan is optimal because every character must be examined at least once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) per test case where L is the length of the string, O(total length) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input string.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){String s=br.readLine();int sum=0;for(int i=0;i<s.length();i++){char c=s.charAt(i);if(c>='0'&&c<='9'){sum+=c-'0';}}sb.append(sum);if(t+1<T) sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
